evals.name: first-login-reward
evals.prompt: Write an Eluna lua script for my AzerothCore server: the first time a player ever logs in on a character, give them 5 gold and a Hearthstone (item 6948), and whisper them a welcome message with their name. Save it as a single .lua file.
evals.expected_output: A .lua file registering PLAYER_EVENT_ON_FIRST_LOGIN (event 30) via RegisterPlayerEvent, callback signature function(event, player), uses player:ModifyMoney(50000) for 5 gold (copper), player:AddItem(6948, 1), and a welcome message via SendBroadcastMessage/Whisper using player:GetName().

assertions.text: Registers the first-login hook via RegisterPlayerEvent with event id 30 (ON_FIRST_LOGIN), not ON_LOGIN
Callback's first parameter is `event`, second is the player (function(event, player))
Grants 5 gold using copper math (ModifyMoney(50000) or equivalent 5*10000), not ModifyMoney(5)
Sends a welcome message that includes the player name via player:GetName()
Output is a single valid .lua file with no invented/non-existent ALE methods

evals.name: gossip-teleport-npc
evals.prompt: I have a custom NPC in AzerothCore with creature_template entry 800001. Write the Eluna lua so that talking to it opens a gossip menu with three options: teleport to Stormwind, teleport to Orgrimmar, and repair all my gear. Make the menu close after picking an option.
evals.expected_output: Lua using RegisterCreatureGossipEvent(800001, 1, ...) for GOSSIP_EVENT_ON_HELLO building the menu with player:GossipMenuAddItem and player:GossipSendMenu, and RegisterCreatureGossipEvent(800001, 2, ...) for GOSSIP_EVENT_ON_SELECT dispatching on intid: Teleport to SW/Org coords, DurabilityRepairAll for repair, and player:GossipComplete() to close.

assertions.text: Registers both gossip hello (event 1) and select (event 2) via RegisterCreatureGossipEvent keyed on entry 800001
Hello handler builds menu with player:GossipMenuAddItem(...) and shows it with player:GossipSendMenu(...)
Select handler dispatches on intid and calls player:GossipComplete() (or GossipSendMenu) to close the window
Teleport uses player:Teleport(mapId, x, y, z, o) with plausible Stormwind and Orgrimmar coordinates
Repair option uses a real durability method (e.g. DurabilityRepairAll), not an invented one
All callbacks include the leading `event` parameter and use only real ALE methods

evals.name: gm-heal-command
evals.prompt: Make a custom chat command .heal for AzerothCore using Eluna. It should fully heal the player who types it and their current target, but only work if the player is a GM. If they aren't a GM, do nothing special. Give me the lua file.
evals.expected_output: Lua registering PLAYER_EVENT_ON_COMMAND (42) via RegisterPlayerEvent, callback function(event, player, command). Checks command == 'heal', verifies player and player:IsGM(), heals player to GetMaxHealth, heals player:GetSelection() target if present, and returns false to consume the command.

assertions.text: Callback signature is function(event, player, command) and matches command == 'heal'
Heals the caster to full using SetHealth(GetMaxHealth()) or equivalent
Heals the target obtained via player:GetSelection(), with a nil check before use
Returns false when the command is handled so the core does not report an unknown command
